microsoft_ft: Owned end-to-end technical execution for **45+ annual resilience drills**, partnering with enterprise customers (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp) to investigate failures, surface recurring defect themes, and drive $14M+ in validated business impact.

Built a **self-service Resilience Automation Platform** from 0→1, defining requirements and scheduling APIs that cut operational toil by 30% and enabled engineering teams to triage and remediate issues without manual escalation.

Delivered Azure's first rack-level proactive testing program in 4 months, achieving a **94% recovery rate** and establishing a repeatable model for continuous validation across backend infrastructure.

Served as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating cross-org triage and communicating resolution status to C-level stakeholders under executive visibility.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — directly feeding capacity insights back into the product roadmap.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of AI-driven code generation tools by running customer-facing demos and training sessions across **14 Azure service teams**, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and surfacing feature gaps fed into the product roadmap.
Conducted **11+ structured user interviews** with technical stakeholders to identify SDK and workflow pain points, directly influencing roadmap prioritization toward intent-based YAML generation.

Improved data retrieval for internal users by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** system with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and reducing low-touch support burden.
microsoft_2022: Drove **$3M in accelerated revenue** by securing cross-functional alignment on an automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams, launching Azure regions 28% faster with fewer escalated support queries.
Partnered with 20+ service teams to diagnose 81 hours of per-region manual toil, then built a roadmap to **automate critical execution paths** and reduce recurring operational support load.
Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to surface automation gaps and support prioritization across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to triage high-impact issues and iterate on process improvements.
amazon_robotics: Defined the legacy OS migration strategy for a **2,000+ unit pilot**, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations to achieve zero operational downtime and ensure seamless backend system continuity.
Triaged and resolved **40+ high-priority tickets** by facilitating Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, stabilizing the developer intake queue and accelerating issue resolution cycles.
Aligned IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, cutting software deployment cycle time by 25% and reducing manual escalation volume to the engineering team.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by managing end-to-end scoping and invoicing for 200+ monthly proposals through an optimized CRM workflow, improving response times and customer query resolution.
Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy with data-driven iteration on web and search profiles.
Identified and sealed margin leaks by conducting **profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, delivering actionable pricing insights that improved leadership decision-making.

tailoring_notes: 'Triage and escalation' language from the JD woven into microsoft_ft bullet 2 and amazon_robotics bullet 2 — mirrors Encord's expectation of investigating issues before escalating to engineering.

'Recurring themes / product roadmap feed' from the JD appears in microsoft_ft bullet 1 (defect themes → roadmap) and microsoft_2023 bullet 1 (feature gaps → roadmap) — directly matches the JD's ask to surface improvement themes to Product.
'C-level stakeholders' from the JD explicitly mirrored in microsoft_ft bullet 4, matching Encord's requirement to manage expectations with technical users and C-Level executives.
'SDK' and 'Python / scripting' keywords from the JD surfaced in microsoft_2023 bullet 2 (SDK pain points) and skills section (Python, REST APIs) — aligns with Encord's Python SDK support requirement.

'Backend systems / logs / APIs' from the JD experience requirements matched in microsoft_ft bullet 2 (self-service APIs, no manual escalation) and amazon_robotics bullet 1 (backend system continuity).
